page: Microsoft Azure D96nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 server family. It is powered by an Intel processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning it relies entirely on external storage options, though it is premium storage capable. There are zero GPUs bundled with this instance, and it does not provide any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware profile is optimized for network performance and features a low memory footprint relative to its high compute core count.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96nls_v6 offers high compute density with 96 dedicated vCPUs while minimizing memory overhead costs by limiting RAM to 2.0 GB per core. This makes it a cost-effective choice for workloads that require substantial processing power and high network throughput but do not demand large memory capacities. Suitable workloads include network appliances, high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and distributed compute tasks that are not memory-intensive.
description: Microsoft Azure D96nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnlv6 family, powered by an Intel x86_64 processor on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 192.0 GB of memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but it is premium storage capable. Designed for high-compute and network-intensive tasks, the server offers qualitative cost efficiency by reducing memory overhead while maximizing processing cores. This resource profile is suited for network appliances, high-throughput web serving, batch processing, and distributed compute workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local disk storage.
